70101.Name: Hero's Herb
70101.Des: After the Asterleeds Independence War, Hero's Herb grew in Ancient City of Vill, spreading towards Asterleeds.

70102.Name: Towering Ruin
70102.Des: It is said to be a divine ruins left by the Bapharia civilization, standing in the center of Asteria Plains.

70103.Name: Statue of the Hero
70103.Des: The Asterleeds Independence War was seen as unjust by the Bapharian Creed. Many tree carvings were spontaneously built by the people to quietly commemorate the heroes who brought independence and peace.

70104.Name: Andra Resting Post
70104.Des: A resting post built after the war, adjacent to the old battlefield of Andra Basin. Some "battlefield scavengers" live here, searching for gear left over from the Asterleeds Independence War.

70105.Name: Andra Landing Battlefield
70105.Des: An untamed and hazardous expanse, once a major battlefield in the Asterleeds Independence War, where wild-growing natural plants coexist harmoniously with war remnants.

70106.Name: Andra Underground Mines
70106.Des: Deep within the Andra Basin tunnels, where the Byrnhald's Giant Golem was destroyed during the Asterleeds Independence War-its abandoned energy core continues to trigger dramatic ecological transformations.

70107.Name: Minsterhorn
70107.Des: Minsterhorn was once a peaceful small village, destroyed by the flames of war during the Asterleeds Independence War. The surviving villagers returned here after the war to rebuild their homes.

70108.Name: Entrance to the Kanamia Tribe
70108.Des: The Kanamia Tribe lives by hunting and gathering, maintaining a non-contact, unwelcoming attitude towards outsiders, with defensive checkpoints set up around the tribe.

70109.Name: Elders' Council Hall
70109.Des: The place where the chieftain and elders of the Kanamia Tribe hold meetings and discuss matters.

70110.Name: Ruins of Andra City
70110.Des: A mysterious ruin, one of the sacred sites of the Bapharian Creed. Seems that humans once lived here long ago...

70111.Name: Dark Mist Fortress
70111.Des: The Dark Mist Legion, which aims to defeat Asterleeds, has established a secret base within the Ruins of Andra City.

90101.Name: Overlooking Fallen River
90101.Des: The Fallen River takes its name from the deep valley it flows through. At the bottom of the valley lies a shallow inlet that displays spectacular scenes.

90102.Name: Thunderfall
90102.Des: The northern highlands are rich in waterways, dotted with countless waterfalls. Fed by melting snow from the mountains, they eventually gather into the Fallen River, nourishing the intricate ecosystem.

90103.Name: Heavensbane Spire
90103.Des: Xeno Engram has reshaped the highland's ecology, causing strange stone spires to erupt from the ground. The sharp rocks pierce upward, pointing straight toward the Pillar of Divinity.

90104.Name: Old Dwelling
90104.Des: The smoke from hearths and the voices of the past have long faded. All that remains of those settlements are silent hollows in the rock walls.

90105.Name: Fallen River Rock Chamber
90105.Des: Hidden within the river's cliff walls is a path leading to an abandoned ruin. Now occupied by monsters, it has become a place where darkness festers.

90106.Name: Inverted Illusory Bloom
90106.Des: The Bahamar Highlands are covered with dazzling crystalline flora known as Illusory Blooms. They are beautiful yet dangerous.

90107.Name: Rock Blooming from a Crevice
90107.Des: According to scholars, these sharp rocks sprouting from crevices display clear growth layers and still continue to grow at an extremely slow rate.

90108.Name: Lallipar
90108.Des: Though the highlands are scarce in resources, Pilgrims constantly come here to worship the Pillar of Divinity. They cultivated crops on this land, and over time, a village took shape.

90109.Name: Pillar of Divinity
90109.Des: As the largest ruin on the Continent of Magna, the Pillar of Divinity is sealed and guarded by the Creed. Though entry is forbidden, countless Pilgrims still come in reverence.

90110.Name: Mine Crane
90110.Des: Influenced by Xeno Engram, the highlands are unnaturally rich in mineral resources. The Duchy of Byrnhald established Sislesa Mine here and stationed troops to oversee extraction.

90111.Name: Field of the Forgotten
90111.Des: Years of excavation have nearly depleted all surface mineral deposits. To maintain output, the Blackstone Legion constructed an underground mining zone.

90112.Name: Mine Pass
90112.Des: Ore from the Sislesa Mine is sent through the mining tunnels to the Bekmar Transport Station for initial sorting, then transported by armored vehicles back to the Duchy of Byrnhald.

90113.Name: Windmill at the Transport Station
90113.Des: As the mine's overseers, the Blackstone Legion stations itself on the nearby hill. The domineering Garrison Outpost looms over the entire mining district like a tower.

90114.Name: Village by the Rails
90114.Des: With miners flooding in, a complete ecosystem gradually formed around the mining area, including stores, eateries, and watchtowers.

90115.Name: Steel Beast of the Transport Station
90115.Des: Raw ore must undergo initial sorting at the Transport Station, and the Squad has built massive, costly sorting machines for that.

90116.Name: Garrison Outpost
90116.Des: The Blackstone Legion stores supplies and trains soldiers here. Terrifying sounds of drills and battle cries often echo from within, and no locals would get near it.

90117.Name: Upper Mustard Tavern
90117.Des: Built against the cliff, the Mustard Tavern is the soldiers' only place of leisure. Its two levels are strictly divided, and the upper level serves only Blackstone soldiers and officers.

90118.Name: Lower Mustard Tavern
90118.Des: The lower level of Mustard Tavern is dim and sunless, with no seating provided. Miners stack crates into makeshift stools just to have a brief rest.

90119.Name: A Corner of the Transport Station
90119.Des: The Transport Station once handled both land and water transport. But as surface mining drained the area's minerals, water levels dropped, and the docks fell into abandonment.

90120.Name: Illusory Bloom Under the Pillar of Divinity
90120.Des: A giant Illusory Bloom near the Pillar of Divinity spreads its vast root system, concealing an entrance beneath it.

90121.Name: The Mending Mines
90121.Des: As the Blackstone Legion's relentless mining extended deeper underground, countless mineshafts were carved out. But once a mineshaft was exhausted, it would be abandoned and forgotten.

90122.Name: Forget, Then Grow
90122.Des: An abandoned mineshaft is like a forgotten scar. Though left unmanaged, the passageways slowly heal over the years, giving rise to new greenery.

90123.Name: Illusory Bloom in the Underground Mine
90123.Des: The Illusory Blooms are drawn to stone. Wherever minerals and crystals are abundant, Illusory Blooms flourish.

90124.Name: Mirage Nappo
90124.Des: Illusory Blooms grow thick throughout the underground tunnels, giving rise to endless rumors and ghost stories. Some claim to have seen Nappo wandering among them in a moment of daze.

90125.Name: Rails in the Transfer Zone
90125.Des: Ore extracted from the underground is temporarily stored in the transfer zone before being transported to the surface.

90126.Name: The Undercity
90126.Des: The Rock Serpents in the underground mines are ferocious, leaving even the Blackstone Legion helpless. All they can do is build heavy Isolation Gates to hold them back.

90127.Name: Deep Mining Zone
90127.Des: The tangled depths of the excavation area are where miners are most likely to go missing.

90128.Name: Crystal Storage
90128.Des: At the very bottom of the underground mines lies a repository for mysterious crystals...

90129.Name: Nature's Return
90129.Des: Even in these dark tunnels, there are places where faint sunlight manages to seep in.

90130.Name: Place of Silence
90130.Des: These dark stone pillars are covered in rough patterns and shrouded in a murky gray, probably once violently damaged.

110001.Name: Starland Town Entrance
110001.Des: The island's sole entrance, featuring a plaza, flowers, sculptures, and giant balloons. It is the starting point of the entire island of dreams.

110002.Name: Aurora Stage
110002.Des: The liveliest spot on Starland, where everyone exchanges moves through dance battles, blending applause with footsteps. It has become the place on the island with the most cheers.

110003.Name: Boarlet Waltz
110003.Des: This Boarlet carousel breaks all expectations and has become a delightfully odd local legend—where everyone can't help but squeal at the adorable little Boarlets...

110004.Name: Eye of the Tides
110004.Des: A massive DJ booth, playing a variety of music, stands on Silver Gull Beach. Decorated with seashells and sunlight, even the tides turn into a melodious tune here.

110005.Name: Cherry Blossom Forest
110005.Des: The blooming Cherry Blossom Forest has become one of the most beautiful spots on Starland. Visitors come here to picnic, enjoy the scenery, take photos, and admire the massive Star Orbiter in the distance.

110006.Name: Star Orbiter
110006.Des: The massive Star Orbiter seems close enough to touch the stars, lifting visitors to the clouds where hidden feelings can finally be spoken. It has become the perfect place on Starland to while away the hours.

110007.Name: Starland Club
110007.Des: On a table no larger than a few inches, you might see grand simulations that decide the fate of nations, or a simple contest settled with a single gold coin. The Starland Club is such a place—fair and just, filled with the cheers of victors and the sighs of the defeated. But what does it matter? It's just a game.

110008.Name: Starlight - The Overlook
110008.Des: Starland's founder, Meowclu, said that the sea and sky get lonely at night too. If we don't hang a lantern high enough, those playful stars might not find their way home. And so, the "Starlight" was built—a watchtower overlooking the entire island.

110009.Name: Heart-Shaped Pond - Not a Wishing Pool
110009.Des: One of Starland's famous landmarks. Its unique heart shape sometimes tempts tourists to toss coins in and make a wish when staff aren't looking.

110010.Name: West Beach - Vacation Vibes
110010.Des: Thanks to Starland's seaside design, the beach and sea here are authentic, making it perfect for visitors seeking a quiet, slow-paced getaway.

110011.Name: Ee-chan Drum Band
110011.Des: One of Starland's main roads, often used as a meeting point. Its enduring popularity may come from being the best spot to watch the Ee-chan Drum Band.

110012.Name: Otherworld Signal Receiver!
110012.Des:
The lights of the Aurora Stage are more than mere illumination—they carry messages.
This idea stems from the bold vision of the founder, Meowclu: if signals from distant other worlds could be captured, just like the aurora itself, then the boundaries of this isolated dream island might one day be broken.

110013.Name: Nappo Minigame Zone - Timely Pay
110013.Des: One of the liveliest game zones on Starland. It hosts various Nappo-themed activities and is the best place to get up close with Nappos. As for whether the Nappos here might suddenly pull out weapons and attack visitors... well, that likely depends on whether the Meowlux Merchant Guild pays them on time.

110014.Name: West Bridge - Parade Track
110014.Des: The original blueprint for the sightseeing train was grand enough to fit half the Merchant Guild, but it was vetoed by Miss Meowclu with the remark, "Carriages would only slow it down." Thus, the Beep-Beep Express retained only its locomotive. Though a little cramped, it lends the journey a touch of unexpected warmth.

110015.Name: West Beach - Sea Breeze Secret
110015.Des:
A corner of West Beach that seems quiet but is actually the densest spot for ghost stories on Starland, thanks to its proximity to the House of Wonders.
If you want to stumble upon someone walking an Arachnocrab or an eccentric detective testing dreams with a tiny vial... come here to discover rumors and ghost stories all your own.
